titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Walpole Family Ephemera Collection
titlestmt.author: by Sandra Markham

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday February 27, 2020 at 02:25

did.abstract: A collection of printed portraits, topographical views, real estate advertisements, maps, and other ephemera associated with the English historian and collector Horace Walpole (1717-1797), his family, and their properties. The majority of the items are plates produced for publications, and over half of the collection is related to two of the family's estates, Strawberry Hill in Twickenham, Middlesex, and Houghton Hall near King's Lynn, Norfolk.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is available for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Marlborough Rare Books Ltd., January 2006.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. People (1746-1884), II. Places (1773-1901), and III. Other Papers (1716-1903).

bioghist.head: Stoke Newington Public Library
bioghist.p: The Stoke Newington Public Library was founded in the early 1890s, in a building constructed with funding from the philanthropist John Passmore Edwards. It is located in the Borough of Hackney, in northeast London.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The material in the collection was formerly owned by the Stoke Newington Public Library in London, and all items have the library's ownership stamp.

In 1905 the library was the beneficiary of a substantial bequest from the antiquarian collector and historian Edward J. Sage, one of the founding library commissioners for Stoke Newington. His bequest included more than five thousand books and a large collection of prints, manuscripts, and ephemera. While it is not certain, it is likely that the material described in this finding aid was part of that bequest as other Walpole-related items in the Sage bequest were purchased at auction and from dealers by Wilmarth Lewis during his lifetime, and by the Lewis Walpole Library afterward. This material can be identified by searching "Stoke Newington Public Library" in the Yale University Library catalog.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Walpole Family Ephemera Collection. The Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of printed portraits, topographical views, real estate advertisements, maps, and other ephemera associated with the English historian and collector Horace Walpole (1717-1797), his family, and their properties. The majority of the items are plates produced for publications, and over half of the collection is related to two of the family's estates, Strawberry Hill in Twickenham, Middlesex, and Houghton Hall near King's Lynn, Norfolk.

Series I holds engraved portraits of Walpole, his father Robert Walpole, other Walpole and Waldegrave family members, and friends such as Mary Berry and Anne Seymour Damer. Series II documents the sale and development of Strawberry Hill and Houghton Hall through printed prospectuses with hand-colored maps and site plans issued by London firms marketing the properties. Also present are engraved views of the houses, a photograph of Houghton Hall taken from Norfolk Photographically Illustrated (1865), prints of Strawberry Hill interiors, and views of homes of Walpole's associates. Series III contains a disparate group of Walpole-related items, including a receipt signed by Horatio Walpole of Wolterton (1716), a manuscript note from Horace Walpole to Joseph Cooper Walker (1792), and a prospectus for Mrs. Paget Toynbee's edition of The Letters of Horace Walpole (1903), among other papers, prints, and photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in alphabetical order by sitter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I holds printed portraits of Walpole and Waldegrave family members primarily created as plates for eighteenth and nineteenth-century publications. Multiple portraits are present for Horace Walpole (1717-1797) and his father Robert Walpole, Earl of Orford (1676-1745), as well as other key associates such as his publisher Richard Bentley and friends Mary Berry, William Cole, and Anne Seymour Damer. The collection also contains a few early twentieth-century photographs of portraits, both printed and painted.

did.unittitle: Hertford, Francis Seymour Conway, Marquess of, 1719-1794

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cut from The Magazine of Art , vol. 8 (1885), p. 126, with Sidney Colvin's article on the portrait drawing by John Downman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wood engraving cut from the Illustrated London News .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plate from Aedes Walpolianae, or, A description of the collection of pictures at Houghton-Hall (London: [1752])

did.unittitle: William Henry, Prince, Duke of Gloucester, 1743-1805

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Home of the Chute family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping from Journal of the Society of Arts about Robert Walpole's home.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Auction prospectus for a sale by the estate of A.F. Lehmann; includes plan of the reception floor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prospectus with folded site plan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prospectus with folded site plan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An article on the house clipped from the Illustrated London News , Saturday, May 21, 1842, and mounted in a booklet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plan does not show development south of Little Strawberry Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plan shows "Proposed New Road" south of Little Strawberry Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plan shows "Waldegrave Park Road" south of Little Strawberry Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two editions of the prospectus: second edition has two additional lots (Lot 2A of freehold land, and Lot 9 of "The Elms") plus conditions of sale and a revised site plan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prospectus with estate plan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes engraved elevations of the South Front and East Front, with frontispiece and plates engraved for A Description of the Villa of Mr. Horace Walpole (Strawberry-Hill: Printed by Thomas Kirgate, 1784).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains plates engraved for A Description of the Villa of Mr. Horace Walpole (Strawberry-Hill: Printed by Thomas Kirgate, 1784).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First edition prospectus for a sale under the direction of the Marquis of Cholmondeley. Includes several site plans, floor plans, and lithographed views of Houghton Hall.

did.unittitle: East and West Fronts, and ground plan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and an offprint.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of an albumen print mounted on card with an engraved caption.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A lithographed view.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The birthplace of Mary and Agnes Berry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains a disparate group of Walpole-related texts and visual materials ranging from a 1716 receipt for tonnage signed by Horatio Walpole of Wolterton to a 1903 prospectus for Mrs. Paget Toynbee's edition of The Letters of Horace Walpole . There is a print view of "The Castle of Otranto," one of the monument to King Theodore erected by Horace Walpole at St. Ann Westminster, and text pages removed from different editions of The Letters of Horace Walpole . Also in the series is a 1792 manuscript note sent from Horace Walpole to Joseph Cooper Walker; its content was published in volume 42, page 384 of The Yale Edition of Horace Walpole's Correspondence (New Haven: 1980) based on an 1885 edition of his letters, but the manuscript itself had been untraced since 1905.

did.unittitle: Printed receipt for tonnage signed by Horatio Walpole (1678-1757)
Manuscript note to Joseph Cooper Walker from Horace Walpole regarding Walker's travels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Yale Edition of Horace Walpole's Correspondence (New Haven: 1980), vol. 42, p. 384

did.unittitle: View of the monument to Theodore, King of Corsica, on a wall in St. Ann Westminster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plate from Antiquities of London and environs , engrav'd & publish'd by J. T. Smith (London: 1798) Etching and aquatint

did.unittitle: "Reminiscences of the Courts of George the First and Second"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from Letters of Horace Walpole, Earl of Orford (London: Richard Bentley, 1840), vol. 1.

did.unittitle: "Short Notes of My Life" and "Memoir Relative to his Income" by Horace Walpole

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from Letters of Horace Walpole, Earl of Orford, to Sir Horace Mann: His Britannic Majesty's Resident at the Court of Florence, from 1760 to 1785 (London: Richard Bentley, 1844), vol. 4.

did.unittitle: "Curiosities of the Peerage" in Chambers's Journal
Page from a Clement Sadler Palmer sales catalogue advertising a Horace Walpole and Strawberry Hill collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a card of the Manchester bookseller Henry Gray.

did.unittitle: Prospectus for Mrs. Paget Toynbee's edition of The Letters of Horace Walpole
"The Castle of Otranto. From an Original Drawing, as it now exists, in the Kingdom of Naples."
